commands.review_summary:
The branch projects runtime tool schemas into provider-compatible shapes, preserves nullable cron validation semantics, isolates cron schema instances, and refreshes affected Codex prompt snapshots.

PR surface: Source +105, Tests +51. Total +156 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main exposes cron nullable/literal composition through runtime tool schemas, and Codex consumes DynamicToolSpec inputSchema as JSON before building Responses API tools. I did not run a live strict-provider turn.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Provider/model-visible compatibility and the protected maintainer workflow need human review; there is no narrow automated repair remaining from this read-only pass.

target.title: fix(agents): project nullable tool schemas for providers
target.body:
## Summary

- Project nullable and literal-union tool schemas into the OpenAPI-friendly provider schema shape used for runtime tools.
- Preserve runtime validation semantics for explicit `null` clear sentinels before provider projection.
- Make cron clear-sentinel fields real TypeBox unions, then project them to provider-compatible plain `string` / `array` / `object` schema shapes for model-facing output.
- Keep cron tool schema instances isolated so provider projection/normalization cannot mutate the raw schema used by validation or later tool instances.
- Refresh only the prompt snapshots still affected after rebasing onto current `origin/main`.

## Verification

- GitHub CI on `ef6bc22dfec` passed: 132 checks passed, 31 skipped, 0 pending, 0 failing.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/llm-core/src/validation.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts src/cron/service.jobs.test.ts src/cron/service/store.test.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed 4 shards / 219 tests under Node 24.15.0
- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check` passed, `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...` passed on changed TS and prompt snapshot files
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json ...` passed on changed TS files
- `git diff --check` passed
- final diff private reported-name scrub passed
- Earlier AWS Crabbox changed gate before the latest rebase: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_cbb1c631efeb`, run `run_4415ced3845b`, command `pnpm check:changed`, lanes `core`, `coreTests`, exit 0.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: strict provider adapters should not receive `anyOf`/`oneOf`/`const`/null-type composition from runtime tool schemas when a provider-safe projection is available; runtime validation should still preserve explicit `null` clear sentinels; provider projection must not poison shared cron schema objects.

Real environment tested: local Codex worktree rebased onto current `origin/main`, final head `ef6bc22dfec`; GitHub CI on that head; earlier AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate on the provider-projection slice.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: rebased onto current `origin/main`; fixed the raw cron schema to use real nullable TypeBox unions; regenerated prompt snapshots; reran focused cron/provider projection tests, the CI-failing cron/MCP tests, prompt snapshot check, formatter, lint, diff check, private-name scrub, and full GitHub PR CI.

Evidence after fix: GitHub CI passed; focused provider/cron proof passed 4 shards with 219 tests; prompt snapshots are current; local lint/format checks passed; earlier remote `pnpm check:changed` completed successfully in run `run_4415ced3845b`.

Observed result after fix: raw cron tool validation accepts `agentId`, `sessionKey`, and `payload.toolsAllow` as `null` clear sentinels, while projected cron schemas expose provider-compatible plain `string`/`array`/`object` shapes and each cron tool instance receives independent nested schema objects.

What was not tested: live Telegram or full assistant-turn E2E; this PR is limited to runtime schema projection, argument validation, cron schema-instance isolation, and prompt snapshot contracts.

## Current CI note

Earlier CI on the stale base exposed branch-owned schema/snapshot drift plus unrelated cron/MCP test failures caused by a moved `origin/main`. The branch is now rebased onto current `origin/main`, pushed at `ef6bc22dfec`, and GitHub CI is green.
